subject: [RFC PATCH 2/5] mm, swap: support zswap and zeroswap as vswap backends

2026-05-28.patch_summary: This patch adds support for zswap and zeroswap as virtual swap (vswap) backends, allowing pages to be compressed or detected as zero-filled without pre-allocating physical swap space. It builds on the existing swap-table infrastructure by introducing virtual swap entries that decouple PTE swap entries from physical backing. The patch only supports zswap and zero-page backends for now, with physical disk backing fallback and writeback of vswap-backed entries disabled until subsequent patches. This change enables more efficient use of swap space and improves performance in certain scenarios.
